run_metadata.coverage_summary: DEGRADED RUN. The session egress proxy returned HTTP 403 (organization policy denial) on the CONNECT tunnel for EVERY WebFetch host this run - verified against all nine Tier-1 portals, all Tier-2/Tier-3 buyer domains, and even a neutral control URL (en.wikipedia.org), and corroborated by the proxy's recentRelayFailures log. WebFetch is therefore unusable; only WebSearch was available, which returns indexed result snippets but not live pages. Consequently NO tender detail page or specification document could be opened, and sources_checked reflects survey-via-search, not pages actually fetched. Tier 1 (all nine jurisdictions), Tier 2 (GBE/public sector/councils/universities/health) and the mandatory Tier 3 (private sector + new-source discovery) were all attempted via WebSearch. Twelve net-new opportunities were captured at HEADLINE level only from public AusTender / Buying-for-Victoria search snippets (real ATM IDs and deep links): ten Commonwealth AusTender ATMs (four Defence DSRG refurbishment RFTs at WA/Mt Waverley/Armidale/Dandenong South; DFAT airport facilitation; DFAT Vanuatu infrastructure adviser; Services Australia Doomadgee agent-services EOI; IHACPA events management; Geoscience Australia geoscience BI; DFAT DEV+ Panel; AACAP airfield design) and one Victorian DoT&P traffic-signal RFT. All are flagged pending-detail and require re-verification once egress is restored. Tier 3 produced strong NEW-SOURCE discovery (18 new sources incl. Workday Strategic Sourcing at APA, SAP Ariba sibling buyers Western Power/Arrow Energy/Energy Queensland, Coupa at Westpac, and TenderLink at Sydney Airport).
gaps: EGRESS POLICY BLOCK (dominant gap): the session egress gateway returned HTTP 403 on the CONNECT tunnel for ALL WebFetch destinations - confirmed for tenders.gov.au, buy.nsw.gov.au, tenders.nsw/vic/sa/tas/act.gov.au, tenders.wa.gov.au, qtenders.hpw.qld.gov.au, tendersonline.nt.gov.au, and every Tier-2/Tier-3 buyer domain, plus a neutral control (en.wikipedia.org). Per the proxy README these are organization egress-policy denials and were not retried/routed around. No detail page or specification document could be read this run. ACTION: the session egress allowlist must permit AU government/buyer domains (e.g. *.gov.au, vendorpanel.com.au, *.tenderlink.com, SAP Ariba/Coupa hosts) before the routine can capture full deliverables/requirements again.

All 12 captured opportunities are HEADLINE-ONLY (from public search snippets): full scope, deliverables, evaluation criteria, contacts, documents and values were not captured. They need a detail pass once fetch access is restored.
AusTender GA2026/818, DFAT-1050 (DEV+ Panel) and AACAP 2026-P04: closing dates not present in snippets; DFAT-1050 may already be in evaluation - status to verify.

QLD QTenders leads seen only via an aggregator (qld2032.com), not QTenders itself, so NOT recorded as confirmed tenders: 'Everton Electorate Office Fitout and Relocation' (Qld Parliamentary Service, ~close 13-Jul-2026) and 'Lease and operate new long day care service - Chinchilla' (Qld Dept of Education, ~close 24-Jul-2026). Verify on qtenders.hpw.qld.gov.au next run.

Tenders Tasmania 'EDSS support/hosting/maintenance' (~close 31-Jul-2026) seen in snippet but likely the same as already-known TendersTAS:edss-2026 - excluded as a probable duplicate pending verification.
NT QTOL: a Dept of Chief Minister & Cabinet tender (close 27-Jul-2026 02:00 PM ACST) appears to exist but title/ID/category were not extractable from the snippet - deferred.

Tenders WA, Tenders SA/Buying4SA and ACT Tenders: no individual current opportunity was extractable via search this run (WA showed only a scheduled-outage notice 1-Jul-2026; SA search was polluted by South-African 'sa-tenders' noise) - deferred.
buy.nsw / NSW eTendering: no net-new NSW opportunity surfaced this run; only previously-known items reappeared in snippets.

SAP Ariba Discovery (service.ariba.com/Discovery.aw): per SAP docs, viewing/responding requires a (free) seller account - it is NOT an open public board. No publicly-visible AU postings capturable without login; recorded as portal-registration only.
Bank procurement (CBA, NAB, ANZ on SAP Ariba; Westpac on Coupa) is invitation/registration-gated with no public RFx board - portals recorded, individual opportunities not captured (no fabrication).

Energy Queensland (Ergon/Energex) public 'Current tenders' pages and the two airport portals (Sydney Airport TenderLink, WSI Airport) may carry publicly-advertised RFx but could not be read due to 403 - highest-value targets to re-attempt once egress is restored.
Tier 2 (GBE/councils/universities/health): no opportunity could be verified because every buyer domain and VendorPanel were 403-blocked; coverage deferred to a future run with fetch access.
recommended_seed_additions: Origin Energy - Origin Vendor Portal (https://www.originenergy.com.au/about/procurement/) - free self-registration, regional SME program.
Western Power - SAP Ariba supplier portal (WA electricity network).
Energy Queensland (Ergon Energy / Energex) - dual VendorPanel(advertise)+SAP Ariba(respond); public Current tenders pages - check weekly.
APA Group - Workday Strategic Sourcing (new platform family; seed for detecting Workday-Sourcing sibling buyers).
Sydney Airport Corporation - TenderLink e-tendering portal (buyer-hosted); seed for other TenderLink sibling buyers.
Arrow Energy and Western Power as SAP Ariba sibling buyers; Westpac as a Coupa sibling buyer (alongside known Qantas).
Lendlease Site Access and John Holland Contractors as subcontractor onboarding portals (packages flow via EstimateOne).
INFRASTRUCTURE: add AU government/buyer domains to the session egress allowlist so WebFetch can open detail pages again - without this the routine can only capture headline-level data.

discovered_sources.source_name: Origin Energy - Procurement / Origin Vendor Portal
discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch of energy-sector procurement; Origin Vendor Portal + Regional Procurement Program confirmed in snippets.
discovered_sources.notes: Free ~30-min self-registration; opportunities surfaced after registration (not a public board). Regional Procurement Program targets regional SMEs. Source/platform identified from web-search evidence; the page itself could not be fetched this run (WebFetch 403 egress block).
